prompt: A high-resolution, analytical photograph of an incredibly vast, steep terraced vineyard (like those in the Douro Valley). The massive mountain range itself slopes sharply and undeniably downward from left to right, dominating the frame as a global downward trend. The hillside is composed of hundreds of separate, narrow, parallel dirt paths (the vineyard rows). Crucially, every single individual path, viewed close up, is built as a steep, distinct upward slope, rising dramatically from left to right. There are no switching or reverse paths; 100% of the paths climb from left to right, creating a clear local trend. Both the parallel upward rows and the overwhelming downward mountain are simultaneously visible and unavoidable. The visual grammar captures a palpable, almost spatial impossibility that feels physically real. Color palette: analytical sage green, rich umber earth, and a cool, distance haze. No text, no arrows, no diagrams. Photorealistic, not surreal.
paradox: Reversal of Trend. A consistent statistical pattern seen in separate subgroups completely reverses direction when the groups are aggregated.
visual_logic: Every parallel terraced row travels uphill (L to R). This is the local trend. The aggregated hillside slopes downhill (L to R). This is the global trend.
